notes.note: A second brief letter of recommendation to the same C. Memmius in exile at Athens. S. = salutem dicit, "sends greetings."

notes.anchor: A. Fufius, one of my closest friends
notes.note: A. Fufius is otherwise little known; Cicero describes him as a learned man (eruditum hominem) of high character, one of his intimate circle (ex meis intimis). The letter asks only that Memmius treat him as he had promised to do in a prior face-to-face conversation.

notes.anchor: in the way you assured me in person you would
notes.note: Roman letters of recommendation typically followed up on prior verbal assurances; Cicero is holding Memmius to a promise made at an earlier meeting, making the letter more a formal record of the commitment than a fresh request.
